TextENGNew: Far away—rising, fading.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: I thought: I'm still here.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: I thought: I'm still me.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: I thought: it's over.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: But of course, it wasn't over.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: I knew that.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: There was still the matter of where to hide what I had done.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: And there was still my father.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: My father, who did not yet know anything—who was probably home right now, humming, making dinner.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: My father, who would believe Rena-san had simply left.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: He would be sad.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: He would be confused.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: But he would be alive.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: He would be safe.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: And that—that was the thing I had done this for.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: I breathed.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: I stared at the ceiling.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: The higurashi sang on.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Far away—rising, fading.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Rising.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Fading.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: I closed my eyes.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: And I rested.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "…That's… all of it, I think."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Rena's confessional drama drew to its close.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: No one applauded. No one called for an encore.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: …Such a quiet, solitary—Rena's entirely solitary—stage.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: I realized I had forgotten to blink for quite some time.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Mion and the others appeared to have done the same.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Rena surveyed us all with an expression that seemed to ask: any questions?
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: When we recognized it as an imitation of Chie-sensei at the end of class, we all answered with small, pained smiles.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "…Just a little while ago, I was over in Yagauchi. Looking for a place to bury the bag. I want to hide it somewhere it will never be found—but ahaha, it's hard to find just the right place."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "I even thought about burying it in our own garden—that it might actually be safer. …But no, that's too risky. Dad might suddenly decide he wants to start a vegetable garden."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "After all, somewhere deep in the mountains is better. I didn't want that woman anywhere near my father—not even in death."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: I was overwhelmed with guilt.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: …Rena's plan had been going flawlessly.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: And I—through my stupid joke, my thoughtless meddling—
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Looking around, it was clear that everyone felt the same remorse.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: If Mion hadn't suggested playing in the trash heap.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: If Satoko hadn't spotted Rena in the trash heap.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: If Rika-chan had managed to stop them from going to the trash heap to play.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: If any of those things had been different—Rena would have spent a few more days, in a way none of us would ever know about, and brought everything to a close on her own.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: And then what?
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: She would have gone on living normally.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: As though nothing had happened.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: As though all those days together—the club, the games, the afternoon at Angel Mort—had never been interrupted at all.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: And we would never have known.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: …That's what she wanted.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: That's what she was trying to protect.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "…You've been carrying all of that, by yourself."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Mion said it quietly. Not reproach—just recognition.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Rena looked down briefly.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "I didn't want to make everyone sad."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "We're not sad."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: I said it before I knew I was going to say it.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Rena looked at me.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "…We're not sad. We're…"
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: I didn't have the right word.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "We're here."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: A long pause.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Then Rena's expression shifted—just slightly. Something in it eased.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "…Keiichi-kun."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "We're here. Whatever happens next—we're not going anywhere."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Mion nodded, very quietly.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Satoko looked down.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Rika-chan said nothing—only watched Rena with those serene, ancient eyes.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: And Rena stood on her rusted stage.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: In the fading light.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "…Thank you."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Her voice was very small.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "Thank you for listening."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: No curtain fell.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: The higurashi went on singing.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: But something had changed.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Something—I couldn't name—had shifted in the air.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Rena climbed down from the car.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: She stood on the ground again, among us.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: And the five of us remained there, together, in the last of the light.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "Welcome, welcome! Step right up—!"
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "Right here! Syrup in strawberry, melon, or Blue Hawaii!"
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "Lottery takoyaki! Come on, come on! Winner gets one packed full of mustard!"
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: As always, the Watanagashi festival had drawn a long row of stalls.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: A festival just needed the sounds of bon odori music and stalls out front, Ooishi thought—that was enough.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Even though it was technically still June, the relentless heat of near-summer days had continued—
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: And with the festival and the bon odori music too, you might as well throw in a fireworks show while you were at it.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "Kumagai here. Ooishi-san, go ahead, Ooishi-san."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: The crackling voice came through the earpiece of Ooishi's portable radio.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: …When Ooishi wore an earpiece, he always looked like he was sneaking a horse racing broadcast during his shift.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "Yes, this is Ooishi. Receiving clearly."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "Uh, sir—the town association has brought over a tremendous amount of food. Oden, yakisoba, motsu stew, and also—um—cold barley tea."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "Nahaha! That barley tea—is it on-duty tea? Or off-duty tea?"
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "Um—ah, ahahaha."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "If it's in cans, please accept it with our thanks. If it's in cups, please decline politely."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "Yes, understood. …Oh, and a representative of the town association is here to pay his respects. The village headman, and also someone from the Sonozaki family…"
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "…O-Rika, the old lady? Oh, I really don't want to—do you think you could manage to tell them Ooishi is out on patrol and you don't know when he'll be back?"
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "…No, sir, they know we have radios and can call you back immediately… she was quite insistent…"
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "Nahahaha! That old woman—cerebrovascular accident, that's how she'll go, mark my words. Understood, understood—please let them know, very politely—and I do mean very—that I'll be right there."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: …Well, so much for that. Time to get back to community relations.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Ooishi ended the communication and made his way toward the festival grounds.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: The Watanagashi festival—the cotton-drifting ceremony—was Hinamizawa's most famous local event.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Dozens of stalls lined the approach to the shrine, and the whole area was packed with people from surrounding villages who had come to watch.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: The grounds rang with children's laughter, the smell of grilled things, the strains of the bon odori music.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Ooishi moved through it all with the practiced ease of a man who had attended this festival many times.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: He was working tonight—the anniversary of the Hinamizawa disaster was always this time of year, and every year since, the village had been watched with care.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "Ooishi-san."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Kumagai appeared out of the crowd, offering a can of barley tea.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "Good man. Thank you."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Ooishi accepted it, cracked it open, and took a sip.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "How's the headman?"
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "He asked me to tell you he appreciated your continued attention to the village."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "O-Rika herself, sir."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: A pause.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "…I see. Lead the way."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "This way, sir."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: They moved through the festival crowd.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Ooishi's eyes never stopped moving—scanning faces, watching the edge of the crowd, noting who stood too long near the shrine path.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: He had been watching this village for years.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: He was still watching.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: He didn't know exactly what he was watching for—he never had, not precisely.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: But he had learned that in Hinamizawa, the things you didn't expect were the things that mattered.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: The Watanagashi night was always—different.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: He couldn't say how. He just knew.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "O-Rika-san is waiting at the shrine pavilion, sir."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "Of course she is."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Ooishi adjusted his tie, finished his tea, and walked on.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: To begin with, Takano-san and I had never had much of a connection.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: We knew each other well enough to exchange nods passing in the street. That was about the extent of it.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: We had only once had a real conversation.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: …And that was recently.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Shortly before the Watanagashi festival.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: A few days after killing Rena-san and Teppei—after Keiichi-kun and the others had found out.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Late one afternoon, I got a call from the library saying my father had forgotten to return a book he'd borrowed.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: My father had gone out shopping at the home center, so I went to return it myself.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: And while I was there, I thought I might borrow something too, and wandered between the stacks looking at random.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: …And that was where I met her.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "Oh—isn't this Rena-chan? Hello."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "Takano-san. Hello. It's unusual to meet you at the library."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "Is it? I find it far more unusual that you're here, Rena-chan. Kukuku."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Her expression held the same composed, adult grace I knew from our brief meetings in the village—not the expression of someone who knew her own death was a few days away.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: She told me she often came here to wait for Tomitake-san.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: To pass the time, she said, and offered to treat me to a drink.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: I had no urgent reason to get home.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: She bought me an iced milk tea from the library's vending machine, and we sat and talked for a while.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: …Looking back now, there was a painful irony in it.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Because she was the one who brought up the Hinamizawa serial mysterious deaths herself.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Without knowing she was about to add her own name to its fifth year.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: She spoke with great enthusiasm about the cases.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: About Oyashiro-sama's curse.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: About the research she and Tomitake-san had been conducting.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Her eyes shone—the look of someone passionately devoted to their work.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "But, you know, I think the truth of this village is far more interesting than a curse."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: She said it with a small smile.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "The pattern of the deaths, the nature of the incidents—there's something very deliberate about them. Very constructed."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "Constructed?"

TextENGNew: "Like someone is maintaining a narrative. Sustaining something."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: I didn't know what to say to that.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "I'm not frightened by it," she added. "Quite the opposite. I find it fascinating."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "But—doesn't it worry you? That something might happen?"
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: She looked at me—directly, calmly.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "Everything that matters involves risk, doesn't it?"
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: She said it quietly, without drama.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: I thought about it afterward.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: I still think about it.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: "Everything that matters involves risk."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: She said that—knowing, perhaps more than she let on, what was coming.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Or perhaps not knowing at all.

TextENGNew: I'll never know.

TextENGNew: We parted pleasantly, outside the library, in the afternoon light.

TextENGNew: "Take care of yourself, Rena-chan."
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: That was the last thing she said to me.

TextENGNew: That was the last thing I said to her.

TextENGNew: And I thought: she is walking toward something I cannot see.

TextENGNew: ● On 'Homing Instinct' and 'Homesickness'
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: In the broadest sense of the term, 'homing instinct' could be said to apply to nearly all living creatures.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: The same holds true for human beings—we instinctively prefer to sleep, rest, and eat in familiar places, in what we call home.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: The home, in this sense, corresponds directly to the nest—and so it can be said that humans too possess a homing instinct.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: But why do animals require a nest in the first place?
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: This too is exactly the same for human beings.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Sleep, rest, and eating are all activities essential to survival—and simultaneously, they are activities that leave one vulnerable.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: It was therefore necessary to establish a 'safe zone' where one could lower one's guard without danger.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: It is widely understood that being denied such a 'safe zone' causes extreme stress.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Forcing an animal into an unfamiliar environment inflicts considerable stress—and for human beings too, being compelled to live in an unfamiliar place, always on guard, inflicts substantial stress.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: This stress is universally recognized as 'homesickness.'
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: In Japan, research into 'homesickness' has been remarkably shallow.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: This is because it has long been defined as a failure of willpower—a laxity of spirit—and its treatment has historically been neglected.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: 'Homesickness' has a very long history. In medieval Europe, it was believed to be not a psychological condition but a physical illness.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Far from their homelands, with no means of hearing a loved one's voice as we have today, young people working abroad fell prey to homesickness in ways that are not difficult to imagine.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: When those afflicted longed desperately to return home yet could not—due to the demands of work—the resulting conflict caused both physical and mental deterioration, and in some cases actual psychological breakdown, driving them to desperate acts of escape or even suicide.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: The people of that era believed in this condition too, and numerous folk remedies and quasi-magical treatments to ward off homesickness were widespread.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Unable to be cured by anything other than returning home, it might rightly be called an intractable disease.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: What is particularly interesting, however, is the following.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Swiss soldiers serving abroad in the seventeenth century—specifically those deployed to France—developed symptoms of homesickness in extreme severity.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: The condition spread among them, and eventually a military surgeon identified it as a distinct disease.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: The symptoms were: persistent melancholy, weeping, loss of appetite, irregular heartbeat, and in severe cases: fever, and finally—death.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: The cause identified by the military surgeon was: a certain melody played on the cowherds' pipes, traditional to the Swiss highlands.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: When Swiss soldiers heard this melody—the sound of their homeland—the homesickness would seize them violently.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: The melody was therefore prohibited.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Soldiers heard playing it, or even humming it, faced severe punishment.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: What does this tell us?
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: It tells us that the trigger for extreme homesickness was sensory: a sound deeply associated with home.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: A sound.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: A familiar, beloved sound—and hearing it, far from the place it belonged, broke something in them.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Now let us consider: what is the sound most associated with Hinamizawa?
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Unquestionably—the cry of the higurashi.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: The higurashi that sings every summer, echoing through the mountains and valleys.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: For those born and raised in Hinamizawa, this cry is not merely a sound.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: It is the sound of home.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: And when someone who carries Hinamizawa within them—in their blood, in their body—hears that cry after being separated from the village—
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: What happens?
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: The same thing that happened to those Swiss soldiers.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: The mechanism is not metaphor.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: It is a hypothesis—and more than a hypothesis.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: The parasite we have identified acts upon the nervous system in a manner consistent with these symptoms.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Stress. Anxiety. Aggression. Paranoia. Deterioration.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: The very symptoms of extreme homesickness—magnified, given a biological vector.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: The cry of the higurashi is the trigger.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Hinamizawa itself is the pathogen.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: And those who carry the village within them—they are both host and prisoner.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: The Queen Carrier maintains the equilibrium.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Remove her, and the balance collapses.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: This is our conclusion.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: This is the truth of Hinamizawa Syndrome.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: The village does not punish those who leave.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: It simply—calls them back.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: In the only language the body understands.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: In the voice of the higurashi.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: In the sound of home.
changeReason: Content misalignment: TextENGNew does not translate the Japanese source text; it appears to contain text from a different part of the story.

TextENGNew: Overcome with envy, one of the onlookers tapped Mion on the shoulder, hoping to share in the royal treatment.
"Ahh, sorry mister, but I'm not a waitress. Could you ask the real waitress over there for your water?"
"Wh-Wh-What?!"
"You won't serve me?!"
"...We're not employees, after all. We have absolutely no reason to serve the likes of you."
"...Satoko, Mii, and I are merely the playthings of the two winners. ...Mii."
"I-I-I can't accept thiiiiis!!!"
Kameda-kun suddenly let out a shriek and rounded on me.
"Can't accept it? What exactly can't you accept?!"
"It's not fair that only K-san gets to have such a delicious club activity! Not fair, not fair, so not fair!!!"
"W-Well, that's... we earned this right by taking on considerable risk ourselves. Even if you call it unfair..."
""W-W-We think it's unfair too!!""
Behind Kameda-kun, Tomita-kun and Okamura-kun appeared, striking dramatic poses!
"...Hmmmm? This is turning into quite an interesting development. What do you say, Kei-chan?"
"What do you mean, 'what do I say'...? What am I supposed to do about it...?"
"All right then, Kameda-kun, Tomita, Okamura.
"If you feel that strongly about it, why don't you challenge Kei-chan and Rena for the winners' privileges?"
"Huh? Mii-chan, what do you mean by that? What do you mean?!"
"These two crushed every other elite member of the club by an overwhelming margin, after all."
"A bunch of nobodies who aren't even members shouldn't stand a chance against them, right?"
Kameda-kun clapped his hands. All right!!
Tomita-kun and Okamura-kun nodded forcefully, brimming with fighting spirit...
Damn it, they expected me to fight these guys, right here and now?!
"Come to think of it, the match between Keiichi-san and Rena-san ended in an unresolved tie, didn't it?"
"...Of course, if you lose here, you'll have to join us in the punishment game."
"...Damn it! I feel like I'm being played here somehow... Rena, what do you think?"
"I won't lose!! Because I'm taking everyone home with me!! I wonder what kind of punishment outfit Keiichi-kun will have to wear~☆"
"I'm taking each and every one of you home!!!"
Sh-She was raring to go...
I could tell Kameda-kun was stoking his fighting spirit, determined to seize the ultimate paradise at his beloved Angel Mort.
The two underclassmen also seemed fired up at the chance to finally battle the club members.
"I, Kyousuke Irie, will be joining this contest as well!! Hyah!!!"
"Hmm, hmm."
"All right! Let's finalize the lineup. Kei-chan, Rena, no objections?!"
"Tch. Objections or not, you already went ahead and decided everything."
"Now, now—it's more fun this way, right? Aha ha ha ha."
"Tomita-san, Okamura-san, if you want to back out, now's your chance. You do understand what happens if you lose, don't you?"
"W-We're prepared for that...!"
"We're men too!
We've always wanted to fight you!!"
They'd witnessed the horrifying punishment games firsthand, and yet they still mustered that kind of courage. ...I had to admit, those two had guts...!!
Sneering past the underclassman duo stood... the Demon Lord of Angel Mort: Kameda!
"Heh heh heh! I never imagined I'd get another chance to fight you, K-san. This time, I'll face you head-on, fair and square!!"
"...Tch, of all the places, I end up fighting Kameda-kun at Angel Mort... In a way, I have a feeling he'll be even harder to beat here than he was in baseball..."
Watching this exchange with a fearless smile was none other than the Prince of Maids: Coach Irie!!
"Heh... Let me show you what an adult's determination looks like."
"And!! Today, right here, I will make real a harem devoted solely to Kyousuke Irie!!!"
"Ahahahahaha! I won't let any of you win!! Hauuu!!"
"...All right!! I've made up my mind. No use griping about it forever. Mion! What's the game?!"
"Heh heh heh! That's more like it!"
"Everyone, clear the way!! Waitresses, please bring it all out!!"
In front of the kitchen, the real waitresses stood at the ready, trays laden with desserts.
So it was going to be... a speed-eating contest?!
"...Um... Manager. Are you really sure about this?"
"Sure, sure, go ahead and bring them out! The more exciting, the better. It's a special event day, after all—we need to liven things up!"
After getting the manager's go-ahead, the waitresses began serving the desserts.
Rena and I faced off against the underclassmen duo, Kameda-kun, and the Coach.
And before our eyes... a staggering mountain of desserts was being lined up, one plate after another.
Ordinarily, I'd have been ogling the waitresses in their outfits as they set down the plates... but this was battle mode now. There was zero room for such indulgence!!
The crowd of spectators around us had whipped itself into a frenzy.
Nobody could have predicted that the event day would turn into something this wild. But nobody cared! As long as it was fun, anything went!

"All right, listen up everyone. This is a contest to see who can eat the most desserts within the time limit. We've already weighed each item, so after it's over, we'll determine the winner by total weight consumed!"
At the words "eating contest," Kameda-kun broke into a grin!
Damn, this only made Kameda-kun's advantage even greater!
"Mion, since we're a duo, could you count the two of us as one person?"
"Hm? Sure, that's fine. You two are always a team anyway. I'll allow it!"
Whoa—in a stomach-capacity contest, having a partner was a huge advantage. Damn!
"How about you, Rena...? Isn't an eating contest kind of tough for a girl?"
"Not at all! If it means I get to take all these adorable girls home, I'd swallow the table and all!!"
...I'd underestimated her. Physical common sense didn't apply to Rena when she was in Cute Mode...
She might happily polish off more than her own body weight in food...!
"Coach... how about you? Any good at eating competitions?"
"...Maebara-san. If there are maids before me, and the condition for claiming them is an eating contest—"
"Then nothing is impossible for me...!!"
The Coach was radiating a sinister dark aura, exuding a fearsome pressure...
The glint behind his glasses was sharp enough to pierce a steel plate!
Damn, so the Coach had a Maid Mode to rival Rena's Cute Mode...!!
Damn it, I had a sinking feeling I was the only one without a special power here...!!
"Is everyone ready?! Set... GO!!"
Before I'd even had a chance to mentally prepare, Mion dropped the starting signal without a moment's hesitation.
Screw it, no turning back now!! Here goes!! Desserts came in all shapes and sizes—some easy to eat, some not! The key was to grab the most advantageous ones before anyone else could!!!
There—what about the yogurt parfait?! Uooooryaaaaaah!!
...But of course, everyone else had the same idea.
Snatch, sna-snatch, sna-sna-sna-SNATCH!!!
The moment everyone reached for the same dish, a white bolt of lightning flashed, and the targeted parfait vanished.
In the empty space where it had been, everyone's outstretched hands collided.
"Ahahahahaha, you're all too slow! I already took it home! Hau!"
Lightning incarnate—that was Rena.
Damn it, I knew it!! There was no way to beat Rena to the draw!!
Rena snatched up the dessert dish like a frog catching a fly with its tongue, and with a tongue equally frog-like, she gulped it down in a single swoop!!
The sheer speed from grab to gulp was so at odds with her usual gentle demeanor that it was genuinely terrifying.
So this was what girls meant when they said there was always a separate stomach for dessert!!!
But in the unique arena of Angel Mort, there was one person who wouldn't yield an inch to Rena—Kameda-kun!!
He grabbed a fruit parfait and, as if chugging a mug of beer, downed it in one magnificent gulp!!
What a spectacularly manly way to eat!!
"...Guhhh. ...Heh heh heh!!"
"It's no use, K-san! You don't have even a one percent chance of beating me in a contest at Angel Mort!! And I'm just getting started!!!"
Even watching those monsters go wild, the Tomita-Okamura duo didn't panic.
After all, there were two of them.
As long as they kept their pace steady, their combined speed was fundamentally fast!
Rena and Kameda-kun were blazing through the early rounds, but if they hit a wall midway, the outcome was anyone's guess...!!
Damn it, I wasn't about to lose either!!
Dig in from the inside, eat, eat, keep eating!!
Augh augh augh! Damn, swallowing ice cream whole made my mouth insanely cold!! How were the others doing this so easily?! ...Hm?
That's it. I just needed to melt it! Melt it with heat and gulp it all down at once!
Plus, if it wasn't cold anymore, my stomach wouldn't freeze up either—which meant I could last longer!!
B-But how was I supposed to melt it?! Body heat would take forever, and I didn't have anything warm...
"Excuse me, waitress, could I place an order?"
"I'd like a hot coffee, please. Preferably served in a pot, if you could."
"C-Coach...!!"
"You're naive, Maebara-san. The idea of melting it with heat—I thought of that, too."
Aaagh, damn it!! The Coach beat me to it...!
Of course, I could still order coffee myself.
But that wouldn't cut it.
I needed to come up with something far faster than the Coach's method!!
"Cool it, Maebara Keiichi. Think. Think, think, thiiiink!!"
Keiichi-kun clutched his head and threw himself backward, shouting his usual catchphrase.
When he gets like that, he says whatever's on his mind out loud, which makes it wonderfully entertaining to listen to.
Oh, but I couldn't let myself fall behind either!!
Eat and eat and eat, and then take all the cuties home!!!
Ahahahahahahaha...☆
I was having the time of my life. My chest was so full of excitement it felt ready to burst.
Sure, the way I was acting right now probably wasn't very ladylike... but I was having so much fun that I couldn't have cared less.
Mii-chan, who dreamed up one outrageous game after another.
And Satoko-chan, the incorrigible prankster.
And Rika-chan—shrewd, but impossible to dislike.
And Keiichi-kun, always so lively, so genuinely fun to be around.
Every day was truly wonderful. 'Happy' felt like such an inadequate word for what I felt.
Sometimes it even made me anxious—was it really okay for every day to be this much fun?
In this world, there are just as many painful and unhappy things as there are joyful and happy ones.
And I know all too well that those things arrive without regard for how hard you've tried or how much you've endured.
Happy days come even without any particular effort.
Unhappy days come even without any particular effort.
But that doesn't mean you can simply stop trying.
I can work to make the happy days last even one day longer, to make them even happier still.
And in the same way—
I can work to make the unhappy days end even one day sooner, to make them even a little less painful.
So I would savor the happiness of these days as fully as I could.
I mustn't be complacent—I mustn't take it for granted that happy days would last forever.
Truly. ...One day, without any warning. Truly.
I knew that these days would come to an end.
So even if... the world were to end tomorrow—I would savor every last drop of today's happiness, so I'd have no regrets in that final moment.
While I was thinking these things, my hands had stopped moving without my noticing.
These fun days spent with my club friends... they filled me with a happiness that threatened to burst my heart.
That was why I even loved the punishment games.
Even those were precious, joyful time.
I had my friends.
I had a place where I belonged.
I had moments where it was okay to just be me.
The me that I loved was right here.
I loved everyone.
Mii-chan, Satoko-chan, Rika-chan, Keiichi-kun. Everyone in our class, the people in Okinomiya—I loved them all, each and every one.
"Ohhh?! Rena's stopped eating! I guess that pace couldn't last forever!"
"This is our chance, Okamura! Now we can overtake Ryuugu-san!"
"Useless useless uselesssss!!"
"If you think you can beat me at Angel Mort, you're sweeter than honey almond tofu!!"
"Rena-san, pull yourself together!! If you let Coach win, I'll never forgive you!!"
"Heh heh heh!! I will not be beaten! If you asked me to drink the Pacific Ocean, I'd drain it dry!!"
"What's the matter, Rena?! Watch this—the great Keiichi-sama's comeback starts right now!!"
"Right, everyone?!"
""YEAAAAAAAHHH!!!""
"...Rena, what's the matter?"
I stood up from my seat.
"Ahahaha, sorry. I just need to run to the restroom for a moment."
I was simply so happy, so full of joy, that my heart was overflowing.
I didn't really care about winning or losing.
Well, of course it was more fun when I threw myself into the competition with everything I had.
But today, this was already more than enough.
If I could crystallize this happiness and keep it... even just a tiny shard of it, I'd tuck it away in my pocket.
And someday, when the cold of difficult days inevitably arrived, perhaps it could be a small warmth to help me endure.
...I was beginning to understand, even on my own.
To feel happiness as happiness—that isn't actually a happy thing.
True happiness is... being so glutted on happy days that you don't even realize you're happy.
The reason you feel happiness so keenly... is precisely because you're trapped in a biting cold.
That's why even the faintest warmth can steal your heart away...
On my way to the restroom, I noticed a waitress and a customer arguing at the register.
The customer was an adult woman.
The waitress was explaining that today was an event day, so entry wasn't possible without a ticket—but the woman kept insisting she hadn't been told any such thing, refusing to back down.
Eventually, unable to watch any longer, the manager stepped in and began apologizing profusely.
"...Honestly. If you can't get in without a ticket, put up a proper sign where people can see it outside. Jeez, what do you take your customers for?"
Our eyes met—that woman, still hurling complaints.
I'd had a feeling it might be her when I heard the voice, so I was mentally prepared.
"...Oh my. Isn't that Reina-chan? What a coincidence, running into you here."
"Oh☆ Hello! Ahaha, what a coincidence, right?"
I held out my hand with a cheerful 'Tei☆,' and she offered her palm in return, letting us share a crisp little high-five.
"Listen, Reina-chan. Can you believe this? They're saying I can't get in because I don't have a ticket."
"Ahahahahahaha, yeah, it looks that way."
"...Well, I suppose there's nothing to be done about it. Hey, even if I can't get a seat, they'll at least sell me a cake to take home, right?"
"Yes, of course, that's no problem at all. Please, right this way—you may choose from any of these."
The manager led her to the cake showcase with a practiced smile.
"I think I'll pick this darling one for myself. And he likes plain ones, like shortcake, if I remember right. Hey, Reina-chan—which one would you like?"
"Me?! You mean it?! Are you sure?! Hauuuuu☆"
"Ahahaha, you really can't resist these, can you? Go ahead and pick whichever you like."
"Um, um! Then... hmmmm......... ...Ahaha, I'll have a shortcake too."
"Oh? Even with all these other adorable cakes to choose from?"
"Ahahahahaha, I know, it's so hard to decide... but when I can't make up my mind, shortcake always ends up looking the most delicious. Hau."
"All right. Then I'll take two shortcakes and one Petit Trianon. 'Taking them hooome~☆'"
She mimicked my catchphrase and flashed me a bright smile.
I answered with a smile just as warm.
Just then, her strong perfume hit my nose, and I was seized by a sudden sneeze.
"Oh my, a cold?"
It seemed Rina-san's perfume and I just didn't get along.
